title: My First Job
summary: A young man talks about his experience starting his first part-time job at a grocery store, describing his daily tasks, his colleagues, and what he learned from working.
body: When I was seventeen, I got my first part-time job at a small grocery store near my school. I worked there every weekend and sometimes on Wednesday evenings. My manager was a kind man called Mr Garcia. On my first day I was very nervous because I did not know what to do. Mr Garcia showed me how to arrange the shelves, how to use the cash register, and how to help customers find things. The work was not difficult but it was quite tiring because I had to stand for a long time. I also learned how to count change correctly when people paid with cash. My favourite part of the job was talking to the customers. Most of them were very friendly and some of them came to the shop every day so I got to know them well. My colleague Maria was very helpful. She taught me some useful phrases to use with customers and always answered my questions patiently. After three months I felt much more confident. I used the money I earned to buy a new bicycle. That job taught me how to be responsible and how to work as part of a team.

annotations.exampleEn: She has a part-time job at the library on weekends.
He stopped at the grocery store to pick up some milk and eggs.
She felt nervous before her first day at school.
It took an hour to arrange the shelves after the delivery arrived.
The cashier opened the cash register to give back the change.
She learned how to count change quickly during her training.
My colleague helped me finish the report on time.
The teacher explained the exercise patiently until everyone understood.
After practising for weeks, he felt confident about the presentation.
Being responsible means doing your tasks without being reminded.

annotations.phrase: work as part of a team
annotations.meaningVi: làm việc theo nhóm, làm việc cùng đội
annotations.exampleEn: The project required everyone to work as part of a team.

grammarNotes.title: Simple past tense for narrative: describing a sequence of events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong các câu chuyện kể về quá khứ, thì quá khứ đơn được dùng để mô tả một chuỗi các sự kiện đã xảy ra. Ví dụ: 'I got my first job... I worked there every weekend... Mr Garcia showed me how to...' Mỗi động từ mô tả một hành động hoàn thành trong quá khứ. Cần chú ý các động từ bất quy tắc quan trọng: get → got, show → showed, teach → taught, buy → bought, feel → felt.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When telling a story in the past, use the simple past tense for a sequence of completed actions. Key irregular verbs in this passage: get → got, show → showed, teach → taught, buy → bought, feel → felt. The simple past is used even for repeated past actions: 'I worked there every weekend' (habit in the past). Compare: 'I work there every weekend' (current habit, present simple) versus 'I worked there every weekend' (past habit, no longer true).

grammarNotes.title: 'how to + infinitive' in reported speech
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'How to + động từ nguyên thể' diễn tả cách làm điều gì đó. Cấu trúc này thường xuất hiện sau các động từ như 'show', 'teach', 'learn', 'know'. Ví dụ: 'Mr Garcia showed me how to arrange the shelves.' — Ông Garcia chỉ cho tôi cách sắp xếp hàng lên kệ. 'I learned how to count change.' — Tôi học được cách đếm tiền thối lại. 'I did not know what to do.' — Tôi không biết phải làm gì.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The pattern 'how to + infinitive' describes the method of doing something. It often follows verbs like show, teach, learn, and know. Examples: 'He showed me how to use the cash register.' / 'I learned how to count change.' / 'I did not know what to do.' — Note that 'what to do' uses a question word instead of 'how'. These structures are very useful for describing skills and instructions.

translationVi: Khi tôi mười bảy tuổi, tôi có công việc bán thời gian đầu tiên tại một cửa hàng tạp hóa nhỏ gần trường. Tôi làm ở đó mỗi cuối tuần và đôi khi vào tối thứ tư. Quản lý của tôi là một người đàn ông tốt bụng tên Mr Garcia. Vào ngày đầu tiên tôi rất hồi hộp vì không biết phải làm gì. Mr Garcia chỉ cho tôi cách sắp xếp hàng lên kệ, cách dùng máy tính tiền và cách giúp khách hàng tìm đồ. Công việc không khó nhưng khá mệt vì tôi phải đứng lâu. Tôi cũng học được cách đếm tiền thối lại đúng khi người ta trả bằng tiền mặt. Phần tôi thích nhất của công việc là nói chuyện với khách hàng. Hầu hết họ đều rất thân thiện và một số người đến cửa hàng mỗi ngày nên tôi quen biết họ rất rõ. Đồng nghiệp Maria của tôi rất hữu ích. Cô ấy dạy tôi một số cụm từ hữu ích để dùng với khách hàng và luôn trả lời các câu hỏi của tôi một cách kiên nhẫn. Sau ba tháng tôi cảm thấy tự tin hơn nhiều. Tôi dùng tiền kiếm được để mua một chiếc xe đạp mới. Công việc đó dạy tôi cách có trách nhiệm và cách làm việc theo nhóm.

questions.prompt: How old was the narrator when he started his first job?
questions.explanation: 'When I was seventeen, I got my first part-time job.'
questions.hint: His age is stated in the first sentence.

questions.explanation: 'a small grocery store near my school'
questions.hint: The location is described in relation to a place he went regularly.

options: Every weekend and sometimes Wednesday evenings
questions.explanation: 'I worked there every weekend and sometimes on Wednesday evenings.'
'My manager was a kind man called Mr Garcia.'
questions.hint: The manager is described as kind.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel on his first day?
questions.explanation: 'On my first day I was very nervous because I did not know what to do.'
questions.hint: He explains the reason for his feeling.

questions.prompt: Which of these did Mr Garcia NOT teach him?
options: How to use the cash register
How to drive a delivery van
How to help customers find things
questions.explanation: Mr Garcia showed him shelves, the cash register, and helping customers — not driving.
questions.hint: Three tasks are listed; one option was not mentioned.

options: Because he had to carry heavy boxes
Because he had to stand for a long time
Because the store was very busy
questions.explanation: 'it was quite tiring because I had to stand for a long time'
questions.hint: He gives one specific physical reason.

questions.prompt: What was his favourite part of the job?
questions.explanation: 'My favourite part of the job was talking to the customers.'
questions.hint: He explicitly names it his favourite.

questions.prompt: What did his colleague Maria help him with?
options: She taught him useful phrases for customers
She introduced him to the manager
questions.explanation: 'She taught me some useful phrases to use with customers.'
questions.hint: Her help was related to communication.

questions.prompt: How long did it take him to feel more confident?
questions.explanation: 'After three months I felt much more confident.'
questions.hint: The time period is stated directly.

questions.prompt: What did he buy with the money he earned?
questions.explanation: 'I used the money I earned to buy a new bicycle.'

questions.prompt: What two things did the job teach him?
options: How to cook and how to manage money
How to be responsible and how to work as part of a team
How to be punctual and how to speak English well
How to lead a team and how to make decisions
questions.explanation: 'That job taught me how to be responsible and how to work as part of a team.'
questions.hint: The final sentence summarises the key lessons.

cloze.template: When I was seventeen, I got my first {{b1}} job at a small grocery store near my school. I worked there every {{b2}} and sometimes on Wednesday evenings. My manager was a {{b3}} man called Mr Garcia. On my first day I was very {{b4}} because I did not know what to do. Mr Garcia showed me how to {{b5}} the shelves, how to use the cash register, and how to help customers find things. The work was quite {{b6}} because I had to stand for a long time. I also learned how to count {{b7}} correctly when people paid with cash. My favourite part of the job was talking to the {{b8}}. My colleague Maria was very {{b9}}. She taught me some useful phrases and always answered my questions {{b10}}. After three months I felt much more {{b11}}. I used the money I {{b12}} to buy a new bicycle.

blanks.explanation: 'part-time' — he worked only some days, not full-time.
'weekend' — he worked every weekend and some Wednesday evenings.
'kind' — Mr Garcia is described as a kind man.
'nervous' — he was nervous because he didn't know what to do.
'arrange' — to arrange the shelves means to put products in order on them.
'tiring' — standing for a long time makes the work tiring.
'change' — counting change means calculating the correct amount to give back.
'customers' — his favourite part was interacting with the people who shopped there.
'helpful' — Maria helped him with phrases and answered his questions.
'patiently' — she answered without getting annoyed, showing patience.
'confident' — after three months he felt more sure of himself.
'earned' — money earned is money received in exchange for work.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says his first job taught him responsibility and teamwork. Do you think teenagers should work part-time jobs while they are still in school? What are the benefits and possible disadvantages for a young person?
